body:
We had an initial implementation of #117 however we subsequently came up with a better UX for dealing with it: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/117#issuecomment-4377092296 

@sepo-agent /answer can you double check the PR and then write down a concrete plan?

comments.body:
I double checked PR #117. My recommendation is: **do not ship it as-is; rework it into the approval-gated diagnosis flow from the latest PR comment.**

What I found:

- Current architecture already has the right hook point: [`run-agent-task`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/actions/run-agent-task/action.yml#L294-L337) captures the agent exit code, does post-processing, then rethrows the failure at the end.
- Failure evidence is already available: [`run.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/run.ts#L232-L250) persists raw stdout/stderr paths on failed runs.
- Discussion primitives already exist: [`discussion.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/discussion.ts#L173-L229) can find Discussions, and [`createRepositoryDiscussion`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/discussion.ts#L342-L352) can create them.
- PR #117 added useful pieces: sanitizer, fingerprinting, duplicate detection, Discussion grouping, and intake auth in [`failure-report.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.agent/src/failure-report.ts#L135-L245).
- But the PR still implements the old UX: `auto`, `true`, `false` only, with public repos auto-reporting by default ([mode resolver](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.agent/src/failure-report.ts#L80-L105), [workflow step](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.github/actions/run-agent-task/action.yml#L364-L443), [docs](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/958f3ffcbe7b39868206a0a35cb0e007c38860ef/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md#L101-L113)).
- The latest accepted direction is instead: `failure -> self-diagnosis -> pending report -> human approval -> central Discussion` ([comment](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/117#issuecomment-4377092296)).
- PR #117 is also currently draft and dirty, so it needs cleanup/rebase before it is mergeable.

Concrete plan:

1. **Split/rebase PR #117 first.** Keep only failure-reporting work in this PR. Remove or split unrelated update-agent/task-timeout changes from the failure-report diff so review stays focused.

2. **Replace the current mode model.** Change `AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_ENABLED` semantics, or introduce `AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_MODE`, with:
   - `false`: no diagnosis or reporting
   - `diagnose`: local diagnosis only
   - `approval`: local diagnosis plus pending central-report draft
   - `true`: auto-publish only when classifier/policy permits

   I would make `approval` the public-repo default and keep private repos at `diagnose` unless explicitly configured.

3. **Add a local diagnosis layer before publishing.** Refactor PR #117’s `failure-report.ts` into two layers:
   - diagnosis/draft generation
   - Discussion publishing

   The diagnosis output should include: category, product-bug likelihood, confidence, sanitized evidence, fingerprint, suggested next action, and proposed Discussion title/body.

4. **Use deterministic v1 classification.** Start with rules based on exit code, route, stderr/stdout patterns, auth errors, provider/rate-limit messages, permission/policy stops, timeout markers, and missing setup. Buckets:
   - `setup_or_auth`
   - `provider_or_runtime`
   - `repo_policy_or_access`
   - `user_task_or_prompt`
   - `agent_product_bug_candidate`
   - `unknown`

5. **Write local pending output.** On failure, `run-agent-task` should write:
   - a GitHub Actions step summary
   - a machine-readable JSON artifact/output
   - a compact local pending-report body when mode is `approval`

   This keeps failure context visible without posting to the central Bug Report Discussion.

6. **Add an approval publish path.** Add a small route/CLI, e.g. `@sepo-agent /report-failure <fingerprint>`, that reads the pending sanitized draft, re-checks requester authorization, then uses the existing Discussion helper path to create/comment in `self-evolving/repo` Bug Report Discussions.

7. **Keep auto-post as explicit advanced mode only.** In `true` mode, publish centrally only if:
   - repo config explicitly enables it
   - the source is allowed by visibility policy
   - the classifier says `agent_product_bug_candidate`
   - confidence passes threshold
   - sanitizer succeeds
   - an intake-capable token is available

8. **Update workflow wiring and docs.** Replace every `vars.AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_ENABLED || 'auto'` caller with the new default/mode. Update config docs, setup docs, action template docs, and PR body so they no longer claim public repos auto-post centrally by default.

9. **Tests to add/update.**
   - Mode resolver tests for `false`, `diagnose`, `approval`, `true`
   - Diagnosis classifier tests for common failure buckets
   - Summary/draft generation snapshot-style tests
   - “approval does not call GitHub Discussions” test
   - “approved publish creates/comments/dedupes Discussion” tests
   - Workflow/action YAML tests proving direct central posting is not default

I’d proceed with this as a rework of PR #117, preserving the reusable sanitizer/fingerprint/Discussion pieces but changing the default UX to approval-gated diagnosis. Please confirm the default should be `approval` for public repos and `diagnose` for private repos before coding.
@sepo-agent /orchestrate let's implement the function from scratch and create a new pr targeting at main

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| orchestrate | implement | Issue #156 | 2 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: agent planner selected implement: Maintainer requested implementation for issue #156 from scratch in a new PR targeting main; the accepted design is the diagnosis/approval flow, not PR #117's direct auto-reporting behavior.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777965953096 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUzNjMzNDM2MTE6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6MTU2OmltcGxlbWVudDoxNTY6Mg -->

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-156/codex-25363409055`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/158

Added a new failure diagnosis/reporting path for failed agent runs: sanitized classification, local step summaries, JSON/pending-report artifacts, approval/default modes, and explicit policy-gated Discussion publishing for `true` mode. Wired the mode through shared workflows and docs, and verified with `npm --prefix .agent test`, YAML parsing, CLI smoke checks, and `git diff --check`.

title: Rethink bug resolution, automatic diagnosis, and automatic reporting
